judgment.summary: Tower Semiconductor shows strong recent earnings growth and a solid analyst consensus, but faces valuation headwinds and high leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter EPS was $0.65 vs estimate $0.56, a 15.7% beat. Next earnings date is 2026-08-03.
positives: Quarterly earnings growth of 62.0% and TTM earnings growth of 62.9% demonstrate strong profitability expansion.
Revenue growth of 15.5% indicates healthy top-line expansion.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter (51.6%) and fiscal year (45.9%) suggest continued strong performance.
The current ratio of 5.63 and quick ratio of 4.75 indicate excellent short-term liquidity.
Consensus rating is strong_buy from 6 analysts with a mean target price of $313.83, suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 121.69 and Price/Sales of 19.29 are very high, suggesting a rich valuation.
Forward P/E of 45.81 and PEG ratio of 7.15 still indicate the stock is priced for significant future growth.
Debt/Equity ratio of 5.24 is high, indicating substantial financial leverage.
Return on equity of 8.7% and return on assets of 4.1% are modest, especially given the high leverage.

news.paragraph: Tower Semiconductor (TSEM) has seen significant positive news recently, including securing $1.3 billion in silicon photonics customer contracts for 2027 revenue and hitting a 25-year high. The company also reported Q1 revenues on target with higher margins, contributing to a substantial stock price increase. This news comes amidst a booming optical stocks sector, though some related companies have seen recent drops.
